reasoning: The question resolves YES if there is at least one day between the open date and June 1, 2025, where NEITHER the New York Times nor the Wall Street Journal contains the word 'tariff' or 'tariffs' on their print front page. While tariffs are a major policy focus for the Trump administration in 2025, the daily news cycle is highly variable. Over a multi-month or even multi-week period, it is virtually certain that other major events (international crises, natural disasters, unrelated domestic politics, or slow news days on weekends) will completely dominate the front pages of both papers. Assuming the probability of the word appearing on at least one front page on any given day is high (e.g., 90%), the chance of it appearing every single day over a 30+ day period is still extremely low (0.9^30 ≈ 0.04). Therefore, the likelihood of there being at least one day without the word is very high.
